page.content: The NCCR research project component includes four categories: Health, Social, and Environmental, with specific degrees and disciplines such as PhD (1) in Epidemiology, MPH (1), and MSc (1) in Environmental Engineering & Management. The titles of the projects are "Health risks of wastewater & excreta reuse in agriculture & aquaculture in northern Vietnam," "QMRA of exposure to wastewater & excreta in agriculture in Hà Nam, Vietnam," "Assessment of human behaviors of reusing wastewater & excreta in agriculture based on PMT Framework," and "Assessing nutrient flows by MFA in Hà Nam, Vietnam." The objectives range from determining infection prevalence (e.g., helminths, E. histolytica) and assessing exposure to wastewater to quantifying nutrient flows and developing scenarios to reduce discharge. Data collection dates span from June – October 2008, April – June 2009, August – July 2010 for the first project, with methodologies including Epidemiology Microbiology and Parasitology.

page.content: Data sources consist of household surveys and wastewater sampling. In stem cell research, Embryonic Stem Cells (ESCs) originate from inner mass cells of blastocysts, with applications in basic science research and limited clinical application. Their markers include SOX2, NANOG, Oct-4, SSEA-1, SSEA-3, SSEA-4 TRA-1-60, TRA-1-81 Frizzled5, derived through isolation from in vitro fertilized embryos. Pros of ESCs are their ability to generate all three germ layers, while cons include ethical concerns and limited source material. Hematopoietic Stem Cells (HSCs) are sourced from bone marrow donations; umbilical cord blood, primarily for transplantation, marked by CD34+, c-Kit−/low, Lin-, CD38-,Flt-3/Flk-2, with pros related to non-controversial sourcing and cons including restricted lineage differentiation and transplantation risks. Induced Pluripotent Stem Cells (iPSCs) are derived from any somatic cell, facilitating basic science research but facing low efficiency of reprogramming, safety concerns, and genetic instability.
